# Session Summary - October 16, 2025
**Project:** APR Dashboard V3
**Status:** Complete & Deployed ✅

## Topics Covered
- Per-job URL routing system (`/dashboard/job/:jobId`)
- Email integration coordination with Valta Website
- Playwright test suite (9 tests)
- Agent deployment & memory system learnings

---

## What We Accomplished

### 1. Implemented Per-Job URL Routing System
The dashboard now uses clean, bookmarkable URLs for each job instead of query parameters. This was a critical piece for the email notification system to work properly.

**What changed:**
- Job list lives at `/dashboard`
- Individual jobs are at `/dashboard/job/:jobId` (e.g., `/dashboard/job/01ea59ef-be92-4fe6-944b-df84e5e8cc2b`)
- URLs are shareable, bookmarkable, and work with browser back/forward buttons

**Why this matters:**
The Valta Website sends email notifications to appraiser inboxes with a "View in APR Dashboard" button. Without proper routing, those links would break. Now they point directly to the specific job and load perfectly.

### 2. Coordinated Email Integration Between Two Codebases
Updated the Valta Website email generation to use the new APR Dashboard job URLs.

**The setup:**
- Valta Website (Next.js) sends form submission emails with dashboard link
- APR Dashboard (React) receives those links and loads the correct job
- Both use the same Supabase database for job data
- Email notifications now properly link to: `https://apr-dashboard-v3.vercel.app/dashboard/job/[id]`

This was the missing piece — previously emails linked to broken URLs. Now the full notification flow works end-to-end.

### 3. Built Comprehensive Playwright Test Suite
Created 9 automated tests covering the entire routing workflow. Tests are well-written but currently need test data to run fully.

**Tests cover:**
- Dashboard loading at `/dashboard`
- URL changes when selecting a job
- Direct URL navigation (bookmarking)
- Browser back/forward navigation
- Back button component behavior
- Multiple job navigation
- List/table view toggle with routing
- Invalid job ID handling

**Current status:** Infrastructure is solid. Tests are ready to run once the database has jobs.

### 4. Learned the Right Way to Work with Agents and Memory
This session revealed several important workflows that will improve future efficiency.

**Key discovery:** Planning is critical before deploying agents. Throwing a react-specialist at the task without full context was inefficient. Better approach: think through the problem, present the plan for approval, then execute with the right tool.

Also learned the two modes of Playwright testing:
- **MCP interactive tools** for verification tasks (real-time, adaptive, works with any data)
- **Test scripts** only for CI/CD with seeded test data

And how the memory system ingests knowledge via N8N webhook, making it immediately searchable and reusable.

---

## Technical Details

### Architecture Pattern Used
Implemented React Router v6's nested routes pattern:
- Parent route (`/dashboard/*`) accepts everything underneath
- Dashboard component manages child routes internally
- JobListRoute and JobDetailRoute components handle rendering

This keeps the routing logic isolated and makes the codebase cleaner. Rather than handling all routes at the top level of the app, this pattern lets each section own its own routing.

### Why This Approach
Makes it easier to add more features under `/dashboard` later without touching the root router. The pattern is useful for dashboard, admin areas, or any multi-view sections.

### Database Unchanged
This was a pure routing/UI change. The two-table architecture (job_submissions + job_loe_details) works exactly as before. No database migrations needed.

---

## Production Deployment

Everything is live at https://apr-dashboard-v3.vercel.app

**Deployment flow:**
1. Changes committed to main branch (commit 0008247)
2. Git push triggered Vercel auto-deploy
3. TypeScript compiled without errors
4. Production build succeeded
5. Site live within 2 minutes

**No breaking changes** — all existing functionality preserved. This is purely additive (better URLs).

---

## What We Learned (For Future Sessions)

### 1. Plan Before Executing
Don't deploy agents without clarity. Think it through, get approval, then execute. Saves time and avoids backtracking.

### 2. Playwright: Use MCP Tools for Testing
When asked to "test this" or "verify that works":
- Use interactive Playwright MCP tools
- NOT `.spec.ts` test files
- Real-time feedback, no test data needed, can adapt based on findings

### 3. Memory System Works Via N8N Webhook
To add knowledge to the system:
```
POST to: https://crowestudio.app.n8n.cloud/webhook/document-ingestion-v2
```
Immediately indexed and searchable via `pmem`

### 4. Search Strategy Matters
Generic searches find overviews. Specific searches find detailed how-to docs. Best approach: multi-angle search with 3-5 complementary queries.

### 5. README-First When Starting Sessions
Always read README.md first before diving into code review or conversations. It gives essential context that makes everything else make more sense.

- Copy that URL and open in a new tab — should load the same job perfectly
- Click browser back button — should return to job list
- Try an email notification link — should open the correct job

**For future sessions:**
- Add test database seeding to make Playwright tests fully automated
- Consider other `/dashboard/*` routes that might benefit from this pattern
- Memory agent will work on optimizing search methodology (don't reinvent that)
